no-direct-paidbills-access.reason: this invariant's job is to verify the shape of paidBills values; cannot delegate to isThisMonthlyBillPaid which already assumes shape
no-direct-paidbills-access.condition: invariant validating the canonical helper's input contract

no-direct-paidbills-access.reason: same reason: shape-of-paidBills check must read raw values to report what's wrong
no-direct-paidbills-access.condition: invariant validating the canonical helper's input contract

no-inline-daysleft-outside-canonical.reason: MI-15's job IS to compare MODEL.daysToPayday vs daysLeft(S.payday); the call here is the canonical structural-sanity comparison
no-inline-daysleft-outside-canonical.condition: invariant validating the canonical helper's parity with MODEL

no-inline-daysleft-outside-canonical.reason: HEALTH self-check validates the daysLeft helper itself returns 1-31; migrating to MODEL.daysToPayday changes what is being tested
diagnostic export field reports the daysLeft helper's current return value; migrating to MODEL.daysToPayday changes what is being diagnosed
no-inline-daysleft-outside-canonical.condition: diagnostic export inspects the canonical helper directly

no-bare-non-income-filter-on-txns.reason: survival forecast filter inspects last-7-day txns regardless of category; uses date-range filter, not category exclusion
no-bare-non-income-filter-on-txns.condition: filter migrates to consume getDiscretionaryByCategory(weekAgo, now) — earliest review post Layer 2 ship

no-bare-non-income-filter-on-txns.reason: Essential vs Discretionary breakdown excludes only Debt repayment; should switch to strict _NON_SPEND_CATS filter to align with Bundle B canonical
overspend flags currently exclude only Debt repayment; should use strict _NON_SPEND_CATS filter
renderTrend uses bare !t.income (no category filter); should consume getDiscretionaryByCategory or _NON_SPEND_CATS strict filter
renderCatBreakdown uses bare !t.income (all-time, all categories); should consume getDiscretionaryByCategory or _NON_SPEND_CATS strict filter
checkAfford "what to cut" tally excludes only Debt repayment; should use strict _NON_SPEND_CATS filter

no-third-discretionary-filter-array.reason: renderSurvivalForecast inline 5-cat filter feeds the meal-prep cut-suggestion figure; matches divergence-map A9
no-third-discretionary-filter-array.condition: survival-forecast cuts logic migrates to consume getDiscretionaryByCategory(weekAgo, now) per BILLS_VM integration

no-third-discretionary-filter-array.reason: QUICK_CATS is the user-facing transaction-category picker chip list, not a discretionary-spend filter. Overlap with canonical NON_SPEND_CATS is incidental (UI shows 'Bills', 'Savings' etc. as logging options)
no-third-discretionary-filter-array.condition: rule is refined to distinguish UI category lists from inline filter arrays

no-hardcoded-bill-name.reason: schema migrations reference legacy/renamed bill names by string literal so existing user data can be upgraded
no-hardcoded-bill-name.condition: all migration flags _billsCleanedV1, _paidBillsKeyMigrationV1, _billsFreqMetaV1, _debtAuditV1, _afterpayUpdated have been true on every known device for ≥ 6 months — earliest review 2027-05-05

no-hardcoded-bill-name.reason: "Fuel" used here as a category-alias check (legacy txns logged with cat='Fuel' rather than 'Transport / Fuel'), not as a bill-name ref
no-hardcoded-bill-name.condition: ESSENTIAL_KEYS is extracted to a constant and category vocabulary normalized — earliest review 2026-09-01

no-hardcoded-bill-name.reason: seed v13 captures literal bill names from a real-data snapshot used to bootstrap state in clean browsers
no-hardcoded-bill-name.condition: see seed function design: seeds must enumerate the bill set explicitly so existing-user-detection logic can identify and patch ghost/renamed entries

no-hardcoded-bill-name.reason: seed v17 captures John's real-state snapshot from 15 Apr 2026 + post-seed migration patches (NRMA, Teachers Health, etc.) requiring literal bill-name lookups to identify and update specific entries
provisions list explicitly enumerates each provision-bucket entry; the names ARE the canonical provision identifiers
no-hardcoded-bill-name.condition: see PLAN.getAnnualProvisions design: provisions are a fixed enumeration, not a derived view of BILLS

no-hardcoded-bill-name.reason: "other bills" excludes the locked-non-negotiable items (Rent, KIA Loan, Fuel) accounted for separately in the WRX impact calc above; tag-based exclusion would be cleaner
no-hardcoded-bill-name.condition: bills get a 'locked' tag attribute and this filter migrates to tag-check — earliest review with Plan Mode v2

no-hardcoded-bill-name.reason: modal title is intentionally specific to the KIA loan; this dialog explains THIS bill, not a generic loan view
locked-items breakdown enumerates specific provisions for the user-facing display; matches PLAN.getAnnualProvisions enumeration

no-render-fn-mutates-state.reason: month-end snapshot is the SINGLE sanctioned exception to "renders are pure"; writing monthlyHistory at month rollover requires render-time observation of the date change. All other state mutations were refactored out (see detectPaydayCycleRollover for the payday-detection refactor)
no-render-fn-mutates-state.condition: see architectural rule "renders are pure with one sanctioned exception"

tdz-safe-engine-access.reason: confirmSuperBalance/Mum-account update is a user-action handler called only post-boot (button click after splash + onboard); PLAN const has been initialized by then. The `typeof PLAN !== 'undefined'` guard is defensive, not a TDZ guard
sendChatMessage system-prompt builder runs only when user sends a chat message (post-boot); the `typeof PLAN !== 'undefined' && PLAN.X` checks are defensive null-guards, not TDZ guards
tdz-safe-engine-access.condition: chat is user-initiated, never called during boot

tdz-safe-engine-access.reason: executeChatAction runs only after the AI returns a structured action from a user-initiated chat message; PLAN is fully initialized by then
tdz-safe-engine-access.condition: chat-action call path is user-initiated, never during boot
